title: ranunculus silvaticus subsp. mixtus
intro: Ranunculus silvaticus subsp. mixtus, commonly known as the lesser meadow rue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Ranunculus, native to Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of silicic acid, which has been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus silvaticus subsp. mixtus include its potential to aid in treating respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be made into a salve or ointment for topical application.
